slides.text: Wow this is a lot of worldbuilding, I wonder if- OH MY- DID THAT REALLY JUST HAPPEN?!
Fonda Lee did an exceptional job of engaging a reader, getting them comfortable in a world so unfamiliar, before finally pulling that comfort all away in a gripping third act.
There are times in the first half of this book where the stakes are lower, but I never felt like it was difficult to get through the story and I loved getting to know the various characters.
The two clans in this book are so similar, yet so extraordinarily different. They, and the characters who represented each, had so many facets.
Just over half way into this book, we immediately jumped into a much higher gear and you could tell exactly when the second act ended.
I'm a sucker for a happy ending, but this book felt like it didn't end in the best way. It felt like a cliffhanger, even if I'm not waiting to find out any specific, I just want more.
